title: React and State Management Libraries Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of Redux, MobX, Recoil/Zustand, and Server State management.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a state management library in a large React app?
options: To provide a central and predictable source of truth for the entire application state.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about Redux?
options: It uses many stores across different components.
It provides a centralized store for the entire application state and follows strict rules for state changes.
It is no longer used in modern apps.
It doesn't require any boilerplate code.

questions.question: Which library uses 'Atoms' and 'Selectors' for granular state management?
Which type of library is specialized in fetching, caching, and synchronizing server data with React components?
Zustand is considered a high-boilerplate and heavy state management solution.
options: False (it's known for being small, fast, and minimal)
